commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Therefore they that speak in proverbs say, Come into Heshbon, let the city of Sihon be built and established: Proverb. Hebrew Moshelim: "Those who speak proverbs, or enigmas, say. "Those were the ancient poets of the Amorrhites, who composed this canticle on the victory of Sehon. (Calmet) Moses inserts it in his work, as an additional proof, that the country was entirely lost to Moab, and as a denunciation of the evils which still hung over the head of that people, and would be inflicted upon them by David, 2 Kings x. , 4 Kings iii. , and Amos i.
